title: Valid Palindrome - Two Pointers Approach
algorithm: Initialize two pointers: left at the beginning (0) and right at the end (length-1)
While left pointer is less than right pointer:
Skip non-alphanumeric characters by moving left pointer forward
Skip non-alphanumeric characters by moving right pointer backward
Convert characters at both pointers to lowercase for comparison
If characters don't match, return false (not a palindrome)
If characters match, move both pointers toward center (left++, right--)
If loop completes without mismatches, return true (valid palindrome)
pseudocode:
ALGORITHM isPalindrome(s)
BEGIN
    left = 0
    right = length(s) - 1
    
    WHILE left < right DO
        // Skip non-alphanumeric characters from left
        WHILE left < right AND NOT isAlphanumeric(s[left]) DO
            left = left + 1
        END WHILE
        
        // Skip non-alphanumeric characters from right
        WHILE left < right AND NOT isAlphanumeric(s[right]) DO
            right = right - 1
        END WHILE
        
        // Compare characters (case-insensitive)
        IF toLowerCase(s[left]) вү  toLowerCase(s[right]) THEN
            RETURN false
        END IF
        
        // Move pointers toward center
        left = left + 1
        right = right - 1
    END WHILE
    
    RETURN true  // All characters matched
END

FUNCTION isAlphanumeric(char)
BEGIN
    RETURN (char >= 'a' AND char <= 'z') OR 
           (char >= 'A' AND char <= 'Z') OR 
           (char >= '0' AND char <= '9')
END

approach:
The Two Pointers approach for palindrome checking is an elegant and efficient solution:

**Key Insight:** A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards, so we can compare characters from both ends moving inward.

**Approach Explanation:**
1. **Two Pointers Strategy**: Use two pointers starting from opposite ends of the string:
   - Left pointer starts at index 0 (beginning)
   - Right pointer starts at index length-1 (end)
   - Move both pointers toward the center

2. **Character Filtering**: The problem requires ignoring non-alphanumeric characters:
   - Skip spaces, punctuation, and special characters
   - Only compare letters (a-z, A-Z) and digits (0-9)
   - Use helper function to identify valid characters

3. **Case-Insensitive Comparison**: Convert characters to lowercase before comparison:
   - 'A' and 'a' should be treated as the same character
   - Ensures consistent comparison regardless of original case

4. **Early Termination**: Return false immediately when a mismatch is found:
   - No need to check remaining characters
   - Optimizes performance for non-palindromes

**Why This Works:**
- If string is a palindrome, corresponding characters from both ends will always match
- By moving pointers inward, we systematically verify the palindrome property
- Skipping non-alphanumeric characters handles the preprocessing requirement efficiently

**Edge Cases Handled:**
- Empty string: Returns true (considered palindrome)
- Single character: Loop never executes, returns true
- All non-alphanumeric: Pointers meet without comparison, returns true
- Mixed case: Lowercase conversion ensures proper comparison

**Optimization Benefits:**
- No additional string creation (unlike reversing approach)
- In-place comparison saves memory
- Early exit on mismatch saves unnecessary comparisons
complexity:
Time Complexity: O(n)
- Single pass through the string with two pointers
- Each character is examined at most once
- Character classification and case conversion are O(1) operations
- Early termination possible for non-palindromes

Space Complexity: O(1)
- Uses only a constant amount of extra space
- Two pointer variables and temporary character storage
- No additional data structures or string copies needed

**Complexity Comparison:**
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ      Approach       в”Ӯ Time Complexity в”Ӯ Space Complexity в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Two Pointers        в”Ӯ     O(n)     в”Ӯ     O(1)      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Reverse & Compare   в”Ӯ     O(n)     в”Ӯ     O(n)      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Recursive           в”Ӯ     O(n)     в”Ӯ     O(n)      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Stack-based         в”Ӯ     O(n)     в”Ӯ     O(n)      в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

**Performance Analysis:**
- Best case: O(1) - mismatch found at the beginning
- Average case: O(n/2) - mismatch found in middle
- Worst case: O(n) - complete palindrome verification needed
- For n = 100,000: maximum ~50,000 character comparisons

**Space Efficiency:**
- No auxiliary arrays or strings created
- Memory usage independent of input size
- Suitable for memory-constrained environments
- Can handle very large strings without memory concerns

**Practical Considerations:**
- Excellent for real-time applications due to O(1) space
- Cache-friendly due to sequential access pattern
- Simple to implement and debug
- Easily adaptable for different palindrome variants

**Trade-offs:**
- Optimal time and space complexity
- Simple and intuitive approach
- No significant disadvantages for this problem
- Industry standard solution for palindrome checking
